ground_truth.title: Houston's Hottest New Restaurants to Visit This Month
ground_truth.author: Sofia Gonzalez
ground_truth.main_content:
Houston has no shortage of great restaurants. From Michelin-recognized and James Beard Award–winning spots to fantastic fast-casual, we have it all. And new restaurants continue to open. While it's challenging to keep up with the city's ever-evolving food scene, Houstonia has compiled a handful of restaurants that have opened this year that are worth adding to your dining bucket list.

 Chateau Bellecru promises a "true French wine bar" experience. Transport yourself to Paris by stepping into its intimate, dimly lit space, then set out for its drinks list. Sip on a selection of wines curated weekly, and pair with something from the food menu. Go classic with a cheese and charcuterie board with a side of truffle chips, or pair with something sweet, like chocolate cake and honey spice bread. Reservations are encouraged for visitors after 7pm.

Eculent Restaurant & Liquid Lab: David Skinner's revival of his esteemed restaurant is back with a refreshed look and menu. Eculent Restaurant & Liquid Lab is perfect for diners yearning for a night filled with theatrical drinks. There's the Sleepy Hollow Cocktail, presented with a steamy bubble that pops before your first sip, and a "mask" drink that requires all the senses to enjoy. Diners can devise their own tasting menu using the required app for ordering, or try the 13-course tasting menu, featuring some of Eculent's favorite bites from over the last decade. Skinner has found a way to use AI to his advantage: Diners can build a profile and order through an app he built, allowing everyone to tailor their experience to their preferences.

Maison Chinoise: This Chinese restaurant officially opened its second Texas location in River Oaks; modern and traditional techniques produce flavorful fusion dishes, including dim sum, dumplings, and house specialties like Beijing duck and roasted spice chicken. Pair its diverse selection of noodles, fried rice, and seafood with drinks like the vodka-loaded Lychee Luck or the pandan espresso martini.

Murray's Pizza and Wine: The team behind Leaf and Grain opened this new restaurant in late October. Start with a refreshing plate of the crudo (yellowtail Hamachi, salmon, leche de tigre, salsa macha, and a kick of Fresno pepper). Then, follow it up with a touch of Italian pies that are perfect for sharing. Signature pizzas include sausage alla vodka, wild mushroom, prosciutto with arugula, and classic cheese or pepperoni. Up the experience with a pick from its list of wines, cocktails, and mocktails.

Ramen Alley: This Asiatown gem elicits the feeling of a South Korea 24-hour convenience store. Equipped with DIY ramen bowls, K-snacks, and K-Pop vibes, diners take their pick from base options including spicy Shin Ramyun, Jin Ramen, seafood soup–flavored Neoguri, and the viral tear-inducing spicy Buldak ramen. Load up on toppings like bulgogi beef, kimchi, cooked bok choy, mushrooms, mozzarella cheese, or even milk for a creamier texture. Cook the ramen on a machine stovetop.

Zaranda: Hugo Ortega is back at it with the opening of his newest restaurant in Downtown, situated just across the street from Discovery Green. The cuisine is what Ortega deems "Las Californias," a reimagining of what the land and its food would look like if there were no borders. The menu combines California's abundance of fruits and vegetables with Baja California's rich seafood, cooking preparations, and wines. Standouts include the tacos de nada, which feature octopus, potato, banana peppers, pickled vegetables, and salsa verde tatemada; the empanadas de Borrego with braised lamb shank, ponzu, and mushroom; and the pato y Conejo, a plate with confit duck leg, braised rabbit, chorizo, saffron, and ancho-morita aioli.
with: Houston has no shortage of great restaurants, from Michelin-recognized and James Beard Award–winning spots to fantastic fast-casual, we have it all.
David Skinner's revival of his esteemed restaurant is back with a refreshed look and menu at Eculent Restaurant & Liquid Lab.
